preFix.title: Hasselback potatoes
preFix.subtitle: Fanned and crisped, butter between every slice
preFix.excerpt: Potatoes cut almost through at 3 mm intervals so they fan out in the oven, with each layer crisped and buttery. A more elegant version of the roast potato.
preFix.sourceNotes: Swedish technique (hasselbackspotatis) adopted into British home cooking. Named after the Hasselbacken hotel in Stockholm.

content.text: Hasselback potatoes look impressive but the technique is straightforward. The only skill involved is the slicing: each potato needs cuts that go almost all the way through but leave a connected base. Two wooden chopsticks or a wooden spoon on either side of the potato act as a guide, stopping the knife before it cuts too deep.

items.name: Potato
items.prepNote: medium, Maris Piper or similar floury variety, peeled

content.text: Heat the oven to 200 degrees C fan. Place a peeled potato on a board. Lay a chopstick or wooden spoon handle on each side of the potato as a depth guide. Slice at 3 mm intervals all along the potato: the chopsticks stop the knife before cutting through the base.

Mix the melted butter with olive oil, garlic (if using), and thyme leaves. Place the sliced potatoes on a baking tray. Brush generously with half the butter mixture, making sure it gets into the cuts. Season with salt.
with the remaining butter. Continue roasting for a further 25 to 30 minutes until the fans are golden and crisp and the base is completely tender.

items.fix: Use two identical-height guides. If a potato breaks, press the halves back together and roast anyway: it still works.
items.cause: The chopsticks were not wide enough to stop the knife at the right depth.
items.symptom: The potato split all the way through during slicing

items.fix: Cuts every 3 mm allow the potato to fan open. Cuts at 5 mm or more will not open as dramatically.
items.cause: The cuts were too shallow or too far apart.
items.symptom: The fans are not opening up during roasting

items.fix: Use a heavy baking tray and line it with foil. If the butter is burning, add a splash of water to the tray.
items.cause: The oven temperature was too high or the tray was too thin.
items.symptom: The butter is burning on the tray before the potatoes are cooked

items.fix: Use medium-sized potatoes and ensure the oven is properly preheated. 200 degrees C fan for 60 minutes is the minimum for a medium potato.
items.cause: The potatoes were too large, or the oven temperature was too low.
items.symptom: The outside is brown but the inside is still hard

content.text: Tuck a thin slice of garlic or a small sage leaf into every third cut before roasting. For a richer version, brush with bacon fat instead of butter. Parmesan grated over the potatoes for the last 10 minutes of cooking adds a savoury crust.

Hasselback potatoes are a Swedish invention from the 1950s, named after the Hasselbacken restaurant in Stockholm. They entered British cooking through food writing in the 1980s. They have since become a dinner-party alternative to standard roast potatoes, valued as much for their appearance as their flavour.
